titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 724

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=960 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:41:10.595819+00 | translation moa=961 status=reviewed modified=2022-11-01 11:41:10.58516+00

p.cl: Ebrardus de Pentkatleht . Omnibus videntibus uel audientibus has litteras salutem .

Nouerit uniuersitas
uestra me de consensu heredum meorum pro salute anime mee et pro animabus patris mei et matris mee
et omnium parentum meorum et pro fraternitate et societate domus dunelmensis quietam clamasse in perpetuum
deo et sancto Cuthberto et Bertramo priori et Conuentui predicte domus Dunelmensis calumpniam quam
habui aduersus eos super terra inter Ederham et Blenerne . et iuri meo si aliquod in prefatam terram
habui  me et heredes meos omnino renuntiasse .

Hiis . testibus . ada de Bonkihil . Patricio filio
ade filii alden . Rollando senescaldo comitis . Henrico de Prendregest . Roberto de Predregest
alano de suinet' . Gregorio senescaldo de coldingh' . Helya de Elmedene . Dauid de Paxtona
Iohanne de Kett' . Ric' . brun . thoma burdun et multis aliis
Everard of Pencaitland, to all who view or hear these letters, sends greeting.

Let all of you know that I have quitclaimed for ever, with the agreement of my heirs, for the salvation of my soul, and for the souls of my father and my mother, and of all my ancestors, and for the fraternity and society of the monastic house of Durham, to God and Saint Cuthbert and Bertram the prior and the convent of the same house of Durham, the claim which I had against them over the land between Edrom and Blanerne, and I and my heirs have entirely renounced my right, if any I had within the aforementioned land.

With these witnesses, Adam of Bunkle, Patrick son of Adam son of Haldane, Roland the earl's steward, Henry of Prenderguest, Robert of Prenderguest, Alan of Swinton, Gregory the steward of Coldingham, Elias of Elmdene, David of Paxton, John of Kettins, Richard Broun, Thomas Burdun, and many others.
